xposts.title: Freedom of will: uncertainty or cheating
xposts.excerpt: Freedom of will is decision making under uncertainty or cheating. If there is no uncertainty, the only degree of freedom is the decision against doing the right thing.

xposts.title: Volition and self as fully automatic
xposts.excerpt: Stare at the human beings until you see how everything they are doing—including volition and experience of self and reality—is fully automatic. It’s not scary, because they are interconnected and you are linked as well

xposts.title: Deconstructing subjective identity
xposts.excerpt: To understand the self, it's necessary to deconstruct our experience of subjective identity, and our experience of what it means for an object to be identical with itself. Identity is a representation, not a reality.

terms.title: Freedom of will
terms.definition: Decision making under uncertainty or the option to act against optimality. In the absence of uncertainty there is no genuine freedom except the choice to deviate from what is right.
